pg38-p1-s1: chap having queer ideas of fun.
pg38-p1-s2: Luck then deserts him and he is slightly injured several times in succession.
pg38-p1-s3:
You would
expect him, if he were normal, to cut it out.
pg38-p1-s4: Presently he is hit again and this time has a fractured skull.
pg38-p1-s5:
Within a
week after leaving the hospital a fast-moving trolley car breaks his arm.
pg38-p1-s6:
He tells you he has decided to stop
jay-walking for good, but in a few weeks he breaks both legs.
pg38-p1:
chap having queer ideas of fun. Luck then deserts him and he is slightly injured several times in succession. You would
expect him, if he were normal, to cut it out. Presently he is hit again and this time has a fractured skull. Within a
week after leaving the hospital a fast-moving trolley car breaks his arm. He tells you he has decided to stop
jay-walking for good, but in a few weeks he breaks both legs.
pg38-p2-s1:
On through the years this conduct continues, accompanied by his continual promises to be careful or to keep off the
streets altogether.
pg38-p2-s2: Finally, he can no longer work, his wife gets a divorce and he is held up to ridicule.
pg38-p2-s3:
He tries
every known means to get the jay-walking idea out of his head.
pg38-p2-s4:
He shuts himself up in an asylum, hoping to mend his
ways.
pg38-p2-s5: But the day he comes out he races in front of a fire engine, which breaks his back.
pg38-p2-s6:
Such a man would be crazy,
wouldn't he?
pg38-p2:
On through the years this conduct continues, accompanied by his continual promises to be careful or to keep off the
streets altogether. Finally, he can no longer work, his wife gets a divorce and he is held up to ridicule. He tries
every known means to get the jay-walking idea out of his head. He shuts himself up in an asylum, hoping to mend his
ways. But the day he comes out he races in front of a fire engine, which breaks his back. Such a man would be crazy,
wouldn't he?
pg38-p3-s1: You may think our illustration is too ridiculous.
pg38-p3-s3:
We, who have been through the wringer, have to admit if we
substituted alcoholism for jay-walking, the illustration would fit us exactly.
pg38-p3-s4:
However intelligent we may have been in
other respects, where alcohol has been involved, we have been strangely insane.
pg38-p3-s5:
It's strong language - but isn't it
true?
pg38-p3:
You may think our illustration is too ridiculous. But is it? We, who have been through the wringer, have to admit if we
substituted alcoholism for jay-walking, the illustration would fit us exactly. However intelligent we may have been in
other respects, where alcohol has been involved, we have been strangely insane. It's strong language - but isn't it
true?
pg38-p4-s1: Some of you are thinking: "Yes, what you tell us is true, but it doesn't fully apply.
pg38-p4-s2:
We admit we have some of these
symptoms, but we have not gone to the extremes you fellows did, nor are we likely to, for we understand ourselves so
well after what you have told us that such things cannot happen again.
pg38-p4-s3:
We have not lost everything in life through
drinking and we
